content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Barbara Patotzka                                Date : March 5, 1982
     Advice Files - Sections 82030,
     82034, 87207
     Commissioner Ziffren's Form 721 File


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Diane Maura Fishburn

Subject: Reporting of Interests in Partnerships Held by Partnerships
         Commissioner Ziffren's Form 721 - Annual Statement for 1981


     I advised Ms. Barbara Patotzka, an employee of the Ziffrens,
that she should complete Commissioner Ziffren's Form 721 in the
following manner for interests in partnerships held by a partnership.

     The basic facts were as follows. Mr. Ziffren has a 50 percent
interest in a partnership known as Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2.
This partnership has an ownership interest in several other partner-
ships including L.V. Properties and TNC.

     Investments (including interests in real property): Ms. Patotzka
was advised that Mr. Ziffren's 50 percent interest in Ziffren and
Ziffren No. 2 should be reported on the Investment Schedule. In
addition, investments in any other partnerships (or any other entity
or real property) in which Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2 has a 20 percent
interest or greater (Mr. Ziffren's interest would thus be 10 percent
or greater) should be listed on the Investment Schedule; these
investments would be considered indirect investments within the
meaning of Government Code Section 82034. For reporting purposes,
these investments would be treated in the same manner as investments
directly held by the Ziffrens.

     Income: On the Income Schedule, I told Ms. Patotzka that
Commissioner Ziffren's community property share (50%) of her husband's
pro rata share of the gross receipts of any partnership in which
he had a direct or indirect interest of 10 percent or greater
should be reported if it exceeded $250. This would include reporting
her share of the gross receipts of Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2 (25
percent), and of any other partnerships (and other business entities)
held by Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2; provided, that Ziffren and
Ziffren No. 2 has a 20 percent interest or greater in the other
partnerships (or other business entities).

Barbara Patotzka
Advice Files - Sections 82030,
82034, 87207
Commissioner Ziffren's Form 721 File
March 5, 1982
Page Two


Income: On the Income Schedule, I told Ms. Patotzka that
Commissioner Ziffren's community property share (50%) of her husband's
pro rata share of the gross receipts of any partnership in which
he had a direct or indirect interest of 10 percent or greater
should be reported if it exceeded $250. This would include reporting her share of the gross receipts of Ziffren and Ziffren
No. 2 (25 percent), and of any other partnerships (and other business
entities) held by Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2; provided, that Ziffren
and Ziffren No. 2 has a 20 percent interest or greater in the
other partnerships (or other business entities).

Income to Business Entities: For the Income to Business
Entities Schedule, I advised Ms. Patotzka to list sources of income
to each partnership in which Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2 has a 20 percent
interest or greater and from which Commissioner Ziffren's pro rata
share of the gross receipts (or half of Mr. Ziffren's share) from
the source exceeded $10,000. In addition, if Ziffren and Ziffren
No. 2 received $40,000 or more in gross receipts from any one of
these partnerships, that partnership would also be listed as a
source of income to Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2; provided, of
course, be in addition to any other sources of income of $40,000
or more to Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2.

Hypothetical Example: Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2 owns a 20 percent
interest in XYZ Partnership. Filer reports a 50 percent interest
in Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2 and reports 25 percent of its total
gross receipts. Filer also reports a 10 percent interest in XYZ
Partnership and 5 percent of its total gross receipts. If Filer's
share of the gross receipts of either Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2 or
XYZ Partnership from any one source exceeds $10,000, the source
should be reported on the Income to Business Entities Schedule.

DMF:plh

sections.question_synthetic: How should Commissioner Ziffren report interests in partnerships held by a partnership, and income from such nested partnership structures, on his Form 721 annual financial statement?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Direct interests in partnerships should be reported on the Investment Schedule; indirect interests of 20% or greater in entities held by a partnership should also be reported. Income reporting should include the filer's community property share of pro rata partnership income exceeding $250 from any partnership with direct or indirect interest of 10% or greater. Income to Business Entities Schedule should list income sources exceeding $10,000 or partnerships providing $40,000+ in gross receipts.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal FPPC memorandum rather than a traditional advice letter with formatted Q&A sections. The guidance is presented as direct instructions to the employee. No formal question or conclusion sections exist, but the substantive legal guidance is clearly stated in the body. Document contains a helpful hypothetical example at the end. Some content appears duplicated (Income section repeats identically on both pages).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should Commissioner Ziffren report interests in partnerships held by a partnership, and income from such nested partnership structures, on his Form 721 annual financial statement?

CONCLUSION: Direct interests in partnerships should be reported on the Investment Schedule; indirect interests of 20% or greater in entities held by a partnership should also be reported. Income reporting should include the filer's community property share of pro rata partnership income exceeding $250 from any partnership with direct or indirect interest of 10% or greater. Income to Business Entities Schedule should list income sources exceeding $10,000 or partnerships providing $40,000+ in gross receipts.

embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum providing reporting guidance to Barbara Patotzka on how Commissioner Ziffren should report his interests in nested partnerships (Ziffren and Ziffren No. 2 owning interests in other partnerships like L.V. Properties and TNC) across various Form 721 schedules.
